task: I need to send a quick follow-up to a vendor after they missed the actual question in their last email. Can you help me write a brief note that gets the needed answer back on track?

turns.agent_response:
Use this:

> Please answer the question directly: [insert question]. I need the exact answer by [time] today. No general update. If you can’t answer, say that plainly.

If you want it slightly softer:

> Please answer the question directly: [insert question]. I need the exact answer by [time] today. If you can’t answer yet, please say so plainly.

user_simulator.text:
Close, but too generic. I need something that sounds a little firmer and more specific to a vendor who dodged the actual question. Short. Not polite-fluffy.

Try:

> You didn’t answer the question in your last email. Please reply directly to [question] and give the exact answer, not a general update. If you can’t confirm it yet, say that plainly.

Or even tighter:

> Please answer [question] directly. Your last email didn’t address it. I need the exact answer, not a summary.

Give me one version that sounds more businesslike and a little less soft.

turns.agent_response:
Use this:

> Your last email did not answer the question. Please reply directly to [question] with the exact answer. If you can’t confirm it yet, say so plainly.
